name: aligned_implies_double_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_1_aligned_implies_double_aligned(addr: nat, size: nat)
    requires
        size > 0,
        aligned(addr, size),
    ensures
        aligned(addr, 2 * size),
{
}
reason: Alignment to size does not imply alignment to double the size (e.g., 4 is 4-aligned but not 8-aligned)

name: odd_aligned_to_even
reason: 1 is not 2-aligned (1 % 2 == 1), so the spec must not entail this

name: alignment_symmetric
code:
proof fn phi_3_alignment_symmetric(a: nat, b: nat)
    requires
        a > 0,
        b > 0,
        aligned(a, b),
    ensures
        aligned(b, a),
{
}
reason: Alignment is not symmetric (e.g., aligned(4,2) holds but aligned(2,4) does not)

name: successor_preserves_alignment
code:
proof fn phi_4_successor_preserves_alignment(addr: nat, size: nat)
    requires
        size > 1,
        aligned(addr, size),
    ensures
        aligned(addr + 1, size),
{
}
reason: Adding 1 to an aligned address breaks alignment for any size > 1

name: aligned_to_sum_of_divisors
code:
proof fn phi_5_aligned_to_sum_of_divisors(a: nat, b: nat, c: nat)
    requires
        b > 0,
        c > 0,
        aligned(a, b),
        aligned(a, c),
    ensures
        aligned(a, b + c),
{
}
reason: Alignment to b and c individually does not imply alignment to b+c (e.g., 6 is aligned to 2 and 3 but not to 5)
